displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: Redemption #5: Master
synopsis:
On the snowy wastes of Rhen Var, Ulic Qel-Droma teaches Vima Sunrider that being a Jedi means facing terrible ordeals and life-and-death choices, warning her against overconfidence even as she playfully bests him. Nomi Sunrider arrives to reclaim her daughter, still angry at Ulic, but Vima insists she is staying because Ulic has already made her a Jedi. Vima vouches for him, showing her mother the ice sculptures of Arca Jeth and Andur Sunrider, and Nomi relents; mother and daughter embrace.

As Ulic finally makes peace with his past, Sylvar arrives seeking to kill him for his crimes and the death of her mate Crado. He refuses to fight, urging her to bury her past as a Jedi should, and deactivates his lightsaber. Realizing he is right, Sylvar frees herself of her hatred and thanks him. But Hoggon, craving glory for slaying a notorious Dark Jedi, shoots Ulic through the chest. As Nomi and Vima tend him, Ulic gives Vima his medallion and tells her to remember the possibilities, then becomes one with the Force. Though he had lost his connection to the Force, Vima says, he understood it and had the heart of a Jedi to the end.
title: Tales of the Jedi: Redemption #5: Master
